hypothesis: In a reactive carbon electrolyzer using the champion conditions (1.5 M KHCO3/0.75 M K2CO3, 3 M K+, 54 °C, 144 mL min−1 bicarbonate flow, 24 mL min−1 KOH flow), applying a 2 Hz, 50% duty-cycle square-wave current pulsing between 0 and 498 mA cm−2 (average 249 mA cm−2) will increase reactor yield by at least 15% relative to steady galvanostatic operation at 249 mA cm−2 by increasing CO2 utilization without reducing JCO.
